data.trigger: New user signs up / completes onboarding
data.goal: Get the user to a validated preference profile, an approved plan, and their first one-tap confirmed order — calmly, building trust.
data.voice_note: Calm, warm, pragmatic. Sentence case. The competent friend who cooks. Every nutrition mention says 'estimate'. Never a diet scold, never a hard sell.
data.cadence: Email 1 immediately; 2 at +1 day; 3 at +3 days; 4 at +6 days; 5 at +12 days.

emails.subject: Welcome — let's get your month sorted
emails.preview: Answer a few questions once, and Rasa handles the rest.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Welcome to Rasa. You already order in most days — Rasa just makes it smart, balanced, and within budget.

Here's the whole idea: you answer a few questions once — diet, goals, what you can spend, what you can't stand — and Rasa builds a 30-day plan that fits your macros and stays under your cap. Then it orders each meal a tap at a time.

One rule we'll never break: nothing is ordered or spent without your tap.

When you're ready, set up your profile — in the app, or paste the prompt into your own ChatGPT/Claude and paste the result back. It takes about three minutes.

[Set up my profile]

Talk soon,
The Rasa team

P.S. Nutrition figures in Rasa are wellness estimates, not medical claims. We keep them honest.

emails.subject: Three minutes now saves you fifteen every afternoon
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

The part that saves you the most is the smallest: telling Rasa about you, once.

Diet and allergens. Your monthly budget. Your protein or calorie goal, if you have one. A couple of things you can't stand. That's it — and from then on, your routine meals are decided for you.

Allergens you flag get hard-blocked before anything reaches your cart. Your budget becomes a hard cap. Your goals shape every pick.

[Finish my profile]

It's the last time you'll think this hard about lunch.

The Rasa team

emails.subject: Your month is ready to review
emails.preview: 30 days, planned to your macros and your cap.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Your 30-day plan is built — meals chosen to fit your macros and stay under your budget, with variety built in so week three doesn't feel like week one.

Have a look and approve it in one review. Nothing is ordered yet — approving the plan just switches on the daily 'tap to confirm' nudges. You can swap any meal in a tap.

[Review my plan]

Every macro figure is an estimate, aggregated across the day. Honest, not clinical.

The Rasa team

emails.send: +6 days (plan approved, no confirmed order yet)
emails.subject: Your next meal is one tap away
emails.preview: We'll show you the dish, the price and the time. You just tap yes.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

Here's how a normal day feels now: around mealtime, Rasa checks the restaurant, the price and whether it still fits — then nudges you.

'Grilled chicken bowl, ₹240, arrives ~12:35 — confirm?'

You tap yes. Five seconds. It arrives. That's the whole thing.

If the price moved past your cap or the kitchen's closed, Rasa offers a fallback instead of ordering anyway. You're always the one who taps.

[Open Rasa]

The Rasa team

emails.subject: It gets better the more you use it
emails.preview: Rate a meal, and the plan learns your taste.
emails.body:
Hi {{first_name}},

A quick thing worth doing: after a meal, tap the rating. Every rating teaches Rasa a little more — better picks, less repetition, closer to what you actually like.

And whenever you want, check your ledger. Planned vs actual, this meal, this day, this month — so the number you set at the start is the number you end on.

That's the deal we're keeping: your meals handled, your macros roughly on track, your spend under the cap — and you always tapping yes.

[See my ledger]

Thanks for being here early.
The Rasa team
